item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An entertaining read, I got attached to Mither. I already had an affinity for mantis shrimp and this one was friendly and helpful! Hopefully those orbs are eggs.

A couple comments I'd like to make, though: first, the face markings felt rather out of place. They didn't appear to serve any purpose and they disrupt the idea of these entities being at least somewhat naturally occurring. Or was that the point? Were the markings a nod to intelligent/deliberate design that the Foundation ignored in its rush to save the world?

+ show block
– hide block

Second, I found the Foundation's naming conventions to be odd. Naming their ships Mither and Teran implies the canonical existence of Orcadian lore… But if that's the case why not call the creatures themselves by those names, since they're clearly the source of the legend? From a meta standpoint I see it as not wanting to be too on-the-nose, but in-universe it seems like a weird choice. Furthermore, if the shrimp has a female face pattern on its back and the eel has a male face pattern on its sides, why was the former dubbed "Homer" and the latter "Angie?" Is this a reference that I missed?

Those are mere nitpicks, but the more substantial issue I had while reading was one of scale. The things are simply so massive that I struggled to comprehend them, which is impressive in its way but mostly I found it disorienting. Even given anomalous lightning cannons it's hard to visualize how the ships serve any purpose given that Angie's mouth opens ten times deeper than the length of an aircraft carrier. It could swallow the whole fleet like so many Flaming Hot Cheetos (hot because of the L-cannons, you see). I'm trying to picture standing on the deck of a patrol ship shooting at something the length of a small island (granted not all of it's above water) and… I don't know, it seems like it would simply skewer your boat with its tail before you were in range. I'm prepared to accept that under my suspension of disbelief but it gives me pause.

Finally, I'd love the article to be clearer about the terms "manifest" and "demanifest." Surely by now the Foundation has observed the phenomenon. Do they burrow out of the ocean floor? Do they simply vanish? What does the appearance or disappearance of a 32km fish do to local sea levels? If Homer can be fitted with holoprojectors then why not a GPS so the fleet doesn't have to sail in circles hoping to bump into it?

I don't mean to be needlessly critical and I'm a mere layperson, not a contributor, but these are things that occurred to me while reading your article and it's precisely because I liked it so well that I took the time to reset my long-lost password and share my thoughts. I look forward to reading more of your work!

Edit: Oh, and a +1, of course.


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn
We did it folks. The Kaiju battles to end all Kaiju battles finally breached the 200 threshold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this! Its well structured, and the ending makes you waiting for more. Its even better since the arthropod is basically an aquatic version of mothra.

item.title: Necropost
item.content:encoded:
The legends indicate that Teran can only be hurt by Mither's weather-based powers and the SCP says conventional weapons don't work on Teran.

With that in mind, the idea that the Foundation would spend the combined total of half of Africa's GDP to devise a weapon that could contain 2 Keters (SCP-3456 and SCP-3700-2), one of which I'm sure is going to be reclassified as Apollyon when the whole thing is done, is totally within Foundation MO.

item.title: Necropost
item.content:encoded:
I didn't think mythology SCPs had much place here. Usually you will get an obscure reference but I've never seen a straight transplant (outside of all of the Jewish stuff and the Monster Manual transplants like Baba Yaga in series 1) that didn't get downvoted into oblivion.

I wonder if anyone is going to take a swing at shinto after this.

item.title: Well shit
item.content:encoded:
I was bored at my inlaws house and decided to read up on Orcadian lore.

This is going to rubber band so hard… can't wait to see what this Apocalypse looks like

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to think the Hiemal dance includes stances that will hurt you regardless of what you decide to do afterwards. Like crawling on the ground with your elbows at a minimum speed of 40 km/h.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I… am very confused about the last bit of the ending. The orbs seem to be a new Sea Mither entity… but I have no idea what the metal thing is. It doesn't fit any of the entry's properties for Teran, nor anything I can find on his mythical properties… and it cuts off at a point where, rather than being tantalizing or furthering the horror, it just leaves me frustrated and confounded.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … There's a Hiemal dance? Does it involve tripping over your own feet and landing painfully?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I read the containment procedures and first paragraph of the description, I thought for sure you had just written Groudon and Kyogre :p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a very good read. Fixed some typos for you as I was reading, BTW.

Follow-up when?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As Bleep said, Lightning Guns were established anom-tech that has been used by the Foundation before.

I don't find this out of character at all for The Foundation. Their entire mission is to preserve normalcy. That doesn't mean "Leave the thing to do whatever" it means 'Do not allow anomalies to change reality in any way'. They're doing that with the most effective means they have.

If you read closely, you'd see that SCP-3700-2 causes progressively worse winters, over and over, if it "wins". This, in my eyes, means significant climate change over the course of multiple losses. That's something The Foundation would definitely be interested in.

As for resources, who cares? In Bleep's Article Canon (the only canon that matters in any given article) The Foundation has the resources to contain 3700. So they did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The lightning guns were pre-established in another piece on the site, and I was merely utilizing a pre-existing anomalous technology which I, and others, happened to be familiar with. Take that as you will, but nothing about this piece is supposed to be entirely realistic, not when you've got Kaiju at play.

I don't really know how to respond to your second point. This is a personal headcanon for you, and one that IMO, doesn't make a whole lot of sense because it fails to understand the point of the article. 3700-2 is a serious threat to normalcy, even at the smaller initial size of 300 m, you're still talking about something the size of three goddamn football field. Its not as if 3700-2 is passive either, it goes out of its way to attack human vessels, vomit Nuckelavees onto the various Archipelagos, and just make things extremely unpleasant, its not something that can be played off as a change in weather patterns. The 800 km stretch of ocean around Orkney, Faroe, and Shetland is pretty high traffic with ferries (as that is the primary means of transportation on and off those islands) and trade freighters. This isn't about making life nicer for the Orkney fishermen, I don't understand where you got that conclusion, or really any of the conclusions you make. We had this discussion in chat, and IMO, people who didn't even like the article came to its defense and explained why your reasoning is short sighted and missing the point, so I'm not going to continue this line of conversation further.

And really, I shouldn't have to defend it any further. 117 people disagreed with you enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unfortunately, there were two things that were a bit jarring for me:

First, the casual introduction of "lightning guns", especially when there's no narrative need for them. You make the rules for this SCP, so you could make it vulnerable to ordinary shells, or at least a specially-designed shell that can be fired from a standard naval artillery battery. If you just wanted to show off how advanced the Foundation was, then naval railguns would have been a much more realistic (e.g. more immersive) alternative.

Secondly, I find the Foundation's intervention to be very out-of-character. I don't see any good reason why the Foundation needed to break the status quo, when the status quo wasn't causing containment breaches and didn't cost the Foundation a ton of resources to manage. Intervening might possibly make winters less harsh for the area, but the Foundation isn't out to make life nicer for Orkney fishermen. Worst-case scenario is a freighter with maybe 20 people on it seeing SCP-3700-2, which would still take far fewer resources to cover up than this massive charade.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love non-Hellenic non-Norse mythology skips. Waiting for exploration log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love these giant monster skips.

Followup when?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is so large-scale and radical I love it
Definite +1. Super excite for the follow-up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All this hard work, and 3 months later, +100. Thank you everyone :')

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved this; the detail, the depth, the concept. Great work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is a follow up coming, and I think people are going to enjoy it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is rad, this is rad, this is rad, this is rad.

Please write the exploration log1, I am begging on my knees.

Honestly, you'll always grab me when gigantic sea monsters and/or obscure myths are involved.

Footnotes
1. Or at least some follow-up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was weird and huge and I don't buy it for a second.

+1 anyway, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see my suggestion for SCP-3700-1 and -2's identifiers were taken, hehehe.

I love how this turned out.~

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm kind of ticked that this ends on a cliffhanger. I hope the follow up to this will deliver.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It hasn't been tagged by the tag team yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aren't the tags missing on this one ?

item.title: New Image Sourcing Post
item.content:encoded: Image created by me in Adobe Photoshop CC 2017, based upon layout found in this section of Google Maps (map data ©2017 Google). I'm releasing it for use in this article and any other CC-by-SA-3.0 usages, as the site's licensing goes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love this. I've only just started to really dive deep (pun intended) into the mythology skips, and this is really, really cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well that was a fun read.

This definitely does have that feel of a Godzilla movie where two Kaiju beat the snot out of one another while humanity (in this case the SCPF ships) are tossed around like so many toys, which helps to make this article so entertaining.

If I was to nitpick, I'd say that you call off the ending a little to early (which is odd considering the length of the piece already). You have the readers in your palm after the two monsters defeat one another, and hint that something else is going on, only to call it there rather than explore it further, which feels a little like pulling the rug out from under us.

Still, the monsters are creative, the action is fun, and that ending incident log really seals the deal (despite ending a little too soon imo).

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the greatest thing ever. I would +1 that image if I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Footage recovered from incident I-3700-039
- +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ending is good. The article feels a bit bloated in places but I think that was a stylistic choice and I appreciated the thorough containment procedures. I never really felt emotionally attached to the proceedings but the sort of epic scale these creatures existed on was more than enough to make up for that. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Erm, yes you are correct, I shall fix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
gamma radiation and high energy photons

Aren't those the same thing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this a lot. My only real issue with it is that, if -2 is really 32km long and with a jaw depth of 3km, I find it unlikely that harpoon guns from battleships would have any effect on its ability to move whatsoever. Assuming it noticed them at all, it would surely be strong enough to either yank them free (ripping the harpoon from the ship entirely), or just submerge and drag all of the ships down with it.
Still, very enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't mind long detailed works, as long as the length and detail are in support of the narrative, here a lot of it feels extraneous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll certainly look into doing some cleanup of the log to remove more confusing/extraneous detail.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sure, I understand the proposed definition. I just don't see it as very necessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fair. I was afraid that the length and detail might turn some people off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: From talking about it in chat with DrBleep, Rimple, and AbsentmindedNihilist, Hiemal refers to anomalies of a grandiose scale that are both self-containing and consist of multiple parts actively containing each other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but I have to admit going cross-eyed during the final battle. I mean, there's just so much going on, all the dialogue about various weapons and designations that I have no context for. It'd be far better with just a simple explanation of what happens, and then maybe that final exchange about both of them being down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't seem that different from other Keters with large geographic scope and military scale containment to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has some very good elements but a lot of unnecessary cruft as well, and I feel is longer than needed for the content. I definitely lost interest halfway through.

The superscience weapons definitely seem unnecessary as does the novel object class. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm honestly not sure about the object class, this is just…so different from most Keters, and different from Thaumiel, that I don't know if it really fits in either category, and Hiemal, based on how Niles described it, feels like the best fit for this article. Before the Foundation really started interfering, the entities were fairly self contained.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, note the "Hiemal" class was very recently invented (after the contest) and only appears on one other article. Honestly I think this article should just be Keter and that other one a Neutralized Keter (with the plant growth thing being a separate SCP entirely) but YMMV.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Think of L-cannons as launching anomalous lightening at a target. It's not technically particle beams, but more in line with electro-lasers I would say. The L-cannons were actually an idea I drew from one of the Resurrection tales, the one where O5-10 goes to each of the other O5 members for votes on MTF-Alpha9, I believe it's O5-7 that is on a battleship with an L-cannon on it, and she explains the mechanics.

Note: I just changed the object class based on an in chat discussion where I discovered that Hiemal is actually a class used to describe two anomalies inherently linked and self containing each other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So "L-cannons" are particle beams or electro-lasers or what? I also don't understand the part about taking in seawater at all.

Why is this Thaumiel? It seems difficult to contain on its own, and the suppression of SCP-3456 seems to be a result of containing this, rather than using this as containment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*does the Hiemal dance*

Ahem. Yeah, this was pretty damn cool, and I hate gulper eels, so the idea of one big enough to actually eat me was terrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved it in the chat, love it here. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like mythology SCPs when they derive from less-well-marketed mythologies. This was most satisfying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've gone through and fixed these things, appreciate it being pointed out. Comma's and its/it's are my worst enemies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Typographical stuff
- Hide 

I can't tell if some of this is intentional, as it's a log but seems unclinically written at points, so I'll just address points I saw.

"The entity's Jaw opens" - Jaw should not be capitalized.

"reach their positions, and" (occurs twice) + "second vocalization, and", "snakes from below SCP-3700-1, and" - commas should not be used before "and" unless both clauses are independent. This occurs several more times throughout the log.

"An audible crack is heard, as the Selkie is released, and the SCP-3456 entity wails".
"seen lifting several crew members, from the Selkie, who were displaced into the ocean, and places them safely"
These are additional commas that may be grammatically incorrect but seem to represent prosaic pauses, so it's unclear if they're correct.

"All vessels, ANGIE", "CENTAUR in the water!", "Selkie, switch targets" - these are the only three lines that represent dialogue from one of the ships that are not enclosed by quotation marks. I would edit these to remain consistent.

"Its too close!" - its should be it's.

"We Read you Delta-7." - Read should not be capitalized.

"Is there any trace of either entity." - Seems like it should end in a question mark to appropriately reflect intent.

"Also Negative" - Negative should not be capitalized.

None of the errors I noted are prevalent outside of the incident log, but the log does strike me as hastily edited. That aside, it's an interesting take on a lesser-heralded branch of mythology with an interesting impact on the region and it's just plain fun to see monsters fighting in the ocean. Outside the errors noted in the log I quite like it, and if the log was tightened up it'd be an easy upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've thought this was awesome right from when I first read it, and it just got better with more and more updates. +1 certainly

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As much as I'm starting to get tired of mythology SCPs, this easily passes the singular most important skip-judging criterion I have: it's fucking cool. So +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had a crap ton of people review this, it's not personal if i forgot to add your name (which I rectified).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see how it is :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There were a ton of people who looked at this and critted this over and over and over and I appreciate their help so much. Shaggydredlocks, Rimple, Roget, Weryllium, DrMagnus, AbsentmindedNihilist, Sterbai, Shio, MrWong, Ninevolt, Salvagebar and a whole crap ton of other people that I shopped this around to helped me immensely in formulating and putting this idea to paper. This was a complex concept, and I've been told several times that this was probably deserving of an 001 proposal.

In reality, this is my lead in to my actual 001 proposal. The concept of the SCP is based on the deities from Orcadian Folklore, the Mither of the Sea and Teran, where the Mither is the arthropod, and Teran in all his might and wrath is the Gulper Eel.

The image is under the public domain, and originates from https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:PSM_V23_D086_The_deep_sea_fish_eurypharynx_pelecanoides.jpg, the original source from which Wikimedia commons obtained the image is https://archive.org/details/popularsciencemo23newy

Show other stuff
hide other stuff

Ok, so you know how I said above that the Mither is the lobster, and Teran the eel? That's sort of correct, but not really. It's more like, the lobster and the eel are pieces of the entities, they are puppets, avatars, physical representations for something much larger, and much more powerful. The Foundation doesn't know this when they set the ball in motion, but by tipping the scales in ones favor they have destabilized what was a natural equilibrium. The lobster and eel annihilating each other was just a small scale event, a pinprick with just enough force to disturb a slumbering entity.
